body_txt: The popular image of a wolf pack involves a group of snarling animals that are always trying to one-up each other. The "alpha" pair are the strongest and most powerful male and female in the group . They have to work to keep their place as "top dog" by frequently fighting with other members of the pack. By extension, this trope is often applied to werewolves , and the use of such a ranking system is also used as a distinguishing feature of other fantastic species. Among werewolves and other sapients, these rankings can act as a Fantastic Caste System . In real life, this was how scientists used to believe wolf packs worked, and for works written during that period, its inclusion is understandable . However, it's since been recognized that wolf packs don't act this way (except in unusual cases where wolves unrelated by blood wind up in a pack together). A normal wolf pack is a family group, usually with the parents in charge just as is usual for people. For more about the history and reality of the trope, see Analysis . Sub-trope to Artistic License – Biology , Canine Confusion , and Greek Letter Ranks . Tangentially related to Alpha Bitch and the Abhorrent Alpha Male , which is similar in concept. No relation to alpha and beta as software terms. Virile Stallion is based on a similar misunderstanding with horses, as it is the mares who lead the herd, not the stallion. This ranking is the idea behind Omegaverse . See also Vigorous Bull , when bulls are depicted as a symbol of masculine pride and are also a symbol of masculinity.
